title: Trail PR Change Packet v0.1.1
description: Canonical JSON Schema for the Trail PR Change Packet v0.1.1. Generated from schema/pr-change-packet.v0.1.1.yml (the authoritative artifact). Consumed by the TS port via Ajv (Phase 1 §7 validation, two-pass: structural + cross-reference). Apache-2.0 (locked at L1 regulatory gate). All v0.1.1 additions are non-breaking vs v0.1; existing v0.1 producers/consumers remain valid.

packet_version.description: Schema version identifier. v0.1.1 is the active Phase 1 protocol; v0.1 is accepted for backward compatibility. If any v0.1.1-only field is present (see §7 schema validation rules), packet_version MUST be "0.1.1".

_meta.description: Packet identity + generator metadata. Phase 1 spec §4 field-level mapping.

packet_id.description: ULID generated at packet finalization (NOT session start). Crockford base32, 26 chars.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 with +00:00 suffix (NOT Z). Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sss+00:00.

version.description: Trail CLI version that produced this packet (from package.json#version of @trail/capture).

schema_url.description: Relative path: "schema/pr-change-packet.v0.1.1.yml" (URL form deferred to v0.2+).

capture_method.description: NEW v0.1.1. "post_hoc" for Phase 1; v0.2 may add "hook".

parent_packet_id.description: NEW v0.1.1 (AB-6). ULID of the prior packet in this session's re-capture chain. Null for the first capture (N=1). Phase 1 reads packet-(N-1).yml._meta.packet_id at write time. Phase 2 UI uses this to compute approval-state carry-forward across re-captures.

pr.description: Pull request context. Populated by simple-git in v0.1.1.

provider.description: MVP: github only; gitlab/bitbucket deferred to v0.2+.

repository.description: "owner/repo". Userinfo MUST be stripped before storage (A4.5 SEC-2; A4.7 R-SEC-4).

base_branch.description: Merge target. Heuristic: origin/main, origin/master, then local main/master. Empty if none resolves.

pr_number.description: Nullable. Populated only when GitHub App ships (v0.2+). Null in Phase 1.

author.description: Git committer email (git config user.email). PII; see §1.1.

task_intent.description: What the work was supposed to do. Phase 1 spec §4.

source_type.description: Origin classification of the task. Phase 1 default: "prompt".

source_ref.description: URL or local ref (e.g., "ISSUE-42", file path, or "PROMPT-001"). Empty if no prompt captured.

summary.description: One-line task summary. ≤200 chars after redact + whitespace collapse.

acceptance_criteria.description: Optional list. Empty in v0.1 (Daniel's solo workflow doesn't always have these).

agent_session.description: How the work was produced. Phase 1 spec §4.

tool.description: MVP: claude-code only; cursor/codex/aider deferred to v0.2+.

model.description: Last assistant model seen in transcript. Kept for back-compat with v0.1; deprecated in favor of models[].

models.description: NEW v0.1.1. Unique model strings, in first-encounter order. Sessions with model switches (e.g., /effort changes) populate multiple.

started_at.description: First ISO 8601 timestamp in transcript. MUST be <= ended_at (parsed as Date, not lex compare).

session_id.description: Filename of the .jsonl minus extension. Always present.

transcript_summary.description: Bullet list, NOT full transcript. Phase 1 mechanical: deduped tool_use names + first sentence of first assistant text block. LLM-augmented when available.

initial.description: REDACTED. Pipeline: tag-strip (<[^>]+>) → empty-check → 1200-char truncate → redact.

followups.description: REDACTED. Same pipeline. Empty-after-strip messages skipped (no PROMPT-NNN allocated).

pattern_set_version.description: Version of bin/trail-redaction-patterns.yml. REQUIRED non-empty; no fallback. Format X.Y.Z (optionally v-prefixed).

pattern_set_origin.description: NEW v0.1.1 (A4.7 R-SEC-1). "bundled" when default trail-redaction-patterns.yml is in use; "user-supplied" when --patterns <path> was passed. Integrity signal — pattern_set_version is user-forgeable when origin != "bundled".

redactions_applied.description: Total count across all captured fields.

validation_errors.description: Layer 2 catches; one entry per pattern (NOT per match). SHOULD be empty in a healthy packet.

skipped_files.description: Files where memory-only snapshot was skipped (size cap; see §9).

diff_summary.description: What changed in the code. Populated via simple-git in v0.1.1.

base_sha.description: Git SHA-1 of the base ref (40 hex chars) or empty when undeterminable.

head_sha.description: Git SHA-1 of the head ref (40 hex chars) or empty.

files_changed.description: GIT DIFF STAT count (NOT count of distinct files touched by tool uses).

modules_touched.description: Heuristic: deduped top-level dirs of changed files INSIDE repo root. Files outside repo (e.g., ~/.claude/projects/...) excluded. Metadata leak; see §1.1.

semantic_changes.description: Human-readable list of WHAT changed. One entry per logical Edit/Write/MultiEdit tool use. IDs MUST be unique (cross-reference rule).

commands_run.description: Ledger of Bash tool uses. NOT full output. IDs MUST be unique. Phase 1: exit_code and duration_ms are constants.

test_evidence.description: Tests detected via bin/trail-test-runners.yml patterns. Phase 1: exit codes locked at 0, so all detected tests land in passed[].

not_run.description: Tests that exist but weren't executed.

provenance.description: Who wrote what. Phase 1 emits constants for the three estimate fields.

method.description: Phase 1: constant "post-hoc-transcript". Heuristic / hook modes deferred to v0.2.

agent_touched_files.description: Files modified by Edit/Write/MultiEdit tool uses (deduped).

human_touched_files.description: Phase 1: always empty (mtime heuristic deferred to v0.2).

summary.description: THE LOAD-BEARING FIELD: claims with evidence refs. Every claim MUST have non-empty evidence_refs (cross-reference rule).

ungrounded_claim_count.description: Populated by Trail; SHOULD be 0 for a healthy packet.

approval_trail.description: AB-4 (NEW v0.1.1). Chronological, per-claim, per-decision history. Phase 1 (capture) emits an empty array; entries are appended by Phase 2 (UI) via the atomic-write saga (B5 §3) and mirrored into libSQL.approval_trail (B5 §7.1). Granularity: ONE entry per (claim_id, decision event); NOT a packet-level singleton. When non-empty, entries MUST be in chronological order; claim_id MUST resolve to a claim in this packet (by id OR stable_id) — verified by the cross-reference pass.

posted_to_pr.description: AB-2 (NEW v0.1.1). Posted-to-PR history. Array (NOT singleton) — a packet may be posted multiple times across re-edits or re-pushes. Mirrors libSQL.posted_to_pr_history (B5 §7.1). Phase 1 emits an empty array; Phase 3b (gh CLI integration) appends entries on each successful post.

redaction_audit.description: AB-8 (NEW v0.1.1). Per-pattern × per-layer breakdown of all redactions applied during this capture run. Mirrors libSQL.redaction_audit (B5 §7.1). Complements (does NOT replace) agent_session.redaction_metadata.redactions_by_pattern aggregate counts — the entries array is the authoritative breakdown by layer; the aggregate map is kept for back-compat and quick rollup.

entries.description: Per-pattern × per-layer rows. MUST be sorted alphabetically by pattern_id, then by layer ascending (parity-locked, deterministic). Sum of entries[].count for layer == 1 MUST equal agent_session.redaction_metadata.redactions_applied (cross-check; Phase 1 invariant).

ulid.description: Crockford base32 ULID — 26 chars, alphabet 0-9 A-Z minus I L O U.

iso8601.description: ISO 8601 timestamp with +00:00 suffix (NOT Z). Format enforced via JSON Schema 'date-time'.

redaction_validation_error.description: Layer 2 catch entry. One per pattern (NOT per match). Snippet is sha256(match).hexdigest()[:8] — exactly 8 hex chars; never the raw match.

snippet.description: 8 hex chars (4 bytes) — sha256 prefix; never raw match.

semantic_change_id.description: Stable ID for a semantic change. Format: DIFF-NNN.

command_id.description: Stable ID for a command. Format: CMD-NNN.

test_id.description: Stable ID for a test entry. Format: TEST-NNN.

claim_id.description: Stable ID for a claim. Format: CLAIM-NNN. Visible ordinal label; deterministic stability key is stable_id (see AB-5).

evidence_ref.description: Reference to evidence (DIFF-NNN, CMD-NNN, TEST-NNN, or PROMPT-NNN). Cross-reference pass (Phase 1 §7 pass 2) verifies each ref resolves to an existing ID.

excerpt.description: Excerpt slice from a tool_use INPUT field. NOT redacted on disk (Decision #3); LLM-prompt boundary IS redacted (A4.5 SEC-3).

kind.description: "before"/"after" for Edit/Write; "before#N"/"after#N" for MultiEdit hunks 1..5 (hunks beyond #5 silently dropped).

text.description: ≤1200 chars; longer content gets elision marker (head=1000, tail=150) — exact: "\n... [elided N chars] ...\n".

semantic_change.description: One entry per Edit/Write/MultiEdit tool use. Single-file scope.

description.description: Tool-specific format: Write→"Wrote {fp} ({char_count} chars)"; Edit→"Edited {fp}"; MultiEdit→"MultiEdit on {fp} ({hunk_count} hunk(s))".

files.description: ALWAYS single-element array (one file per DIFF). Locked v0.1.1.

command.description: One entry per Bash tool use. command + stdout_summary REDACTED before write.
≤500 chars before redaction; REDACTED before write.

stdout_summary.description: ≤1200 chars before redaction; REDACTED before write.

stderr_summary.description: ALWAYS EMPTY in Phase 1 (Claude Code mixes stderr into result text; split deferred to v0.2).

test_entry.description: Test detection entry. ref is 140-char-truncated bash command, then redacted.

ref.description: ≤140 chars (truncated bash command), then redacted (A4.5 SEC-1).

cmd_ref.description: NEW v0.1.1. Back-pointer to commands_run entry. Cross-reference pass verifies it resolves.

claim_risk_classification.description: AB-1 / AB-1a (NEW v0.1.1; additive, all sub-fields nullable). Per-claim risk metadata. Phase 1 emits structure with all fields null so Phase 2 (UI) can populate creator_override / reviewer_override without a schema migration.

agent.description: Agent's classification. DEFERRED to v0.2 — Phase 1 emits null block.

creator_override.description: AB-1: claim-level override by the PR creator.

risk_classification_block.description: Agent's classification sub-block. Phase 1: both fields null.

risk_override_block.description: Override sub-block (creator or reviewer). When level is non-null, reason/at/by MUST also be non-null (validated at the consumer; not expressible cleanly in pure JSON Schema without dependentRequired across nullable fields).

level.description: Null = not overridden. Otherwise risk_level enum.

at.description: ISO 8601. Required (non-null) when level is non-null.

by.description: User identity (email/handle). Required (non-null) when level is non-null.

approval_trail_entry.description: AB-4 (NEW v0.1.1). One row per (claim_id, decision event). claim_id values appended by Phase 2 reference the stable claim identifier — when stable_id is populated, Phase 2 SHOULD write stable_id; otherwise the human-facing CLAIM-NNN id (transitional). libSQL's approval_trail.claim_id column joins to claims.claim_id (which is stable_id post-AB-5).

claim_id.description: Either the human-facing CLAIM-NNN id OR the claim's 16-hex stable_id (AB-5). Cross-reference pass verifies it resolves to a claim in this packet.

reason.description: Optional rationale (≤500 chars after redact). Nullable string.

at.description: ISO 8601 with +00:00 suffix. Entries MUST be in chronological order (validated at the consumer; not expressible cleanly in pure JSON Schema).

posted_to_pr_entry.description: AB-2 (NEW v0.1.1). One row per successful post to a PR. body_hash is sha256 (full hex, deterministic) of the markdown that was posted.

pr_url.description: Full URL to the pull request.

body_hash.description: sha256 of the markdown that was posted (full hex; deterministic). 64 lowercase hex chars.

posted_by.description: User identity that triggered the post (email/handle).

redaction_audit_entry.description: AB-8 (NEW v0.1.1). Per-pattern × per-layer row. NEVER includes raw match content (use 8-char-hex snippets if precision needed — same locking as redaction_validation_error).

layer.description: 1 = Layer 1 capture-time; 2 = Layer 2 write-time; 3 = Layer 3 pre-commit (populated only when Phase 3a ships).

count.description: Match count for this (pattern_id, layer) tuple.

locations_summary.description: Human-readable summary of where matches were found; NEVER includes raw match content.

claim.description: A single claim with evidence refs. THE LOAD-BEARING SHAPE: every claim MUST have non-empty evidence_refs (Phase 1 §7 cross-reference pass).

stable_id.description: AB-5 (NEW v0.1.1). 16-hex-char deterministic key. sha256(session_id || '|' || claim_text || '|' || position) truncated to 16 hex chars. Stable across re-captures of the same session; consumers (libSQL claims.claim_id, Phase 2 carry-forward) MUST key by stable_id, NOT by id.

evidence_refs.description: Non-empty array of evidence references (DIFF-NNN, CMD-NNN, TEST-NNN, PROMPT-NNN). Cross-reference pass (§7 pass 2) verifies each resolves.

synthesis_mode.description: NEW v0.1.1. Per-claim synthesis source. Mixed-mode runs are normal: LLM mode appends a mechanical "Ran N test/validation command(s)" rollup.
